description_long: This is a large, historical-style mortar cannon mounted on a robust wooden carriage with four cylindrical wheels. The main body of the mortar is constructed from heavy, dark metal, featuring a wide, short barrel angled upward for high-arc firing. The carriage is reinforced with metal bands and bolts, and the wheels are capped with metal for durability. Several metal rings and rods are attached to the sides, likely for maneuvering or securing the weapon. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with visible wear and a matte, slightly weathered finish. The design suggests it was intended for siege or defensive use, capable of launching large projectiles over obstacles.
description: A heavy, metal mortar cannon on a reinforced wooden carriage with four metal-capped wheels and utilitarian fittings. The barrel is short, wide, and angled for high-arc firing.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide, circular opening of the mortar barrel and the sturdy, metal-capped wheels.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the angled barrel, thick wooden carriage, and metal reinforcement bands.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the carriage, the closed end of the barrel, and the symmetrical wheel arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals additional metal fittings and the angled barrel profile.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One large fixed mortar barrel mounted centrally on the carriage; no additional mounts visible.
attribute_armor_plating: No dedicated armor plating visible; carriage and barrel are constructed from thick metal and wood for structural strength.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; surfaces are solid gray and dark brown with no discernible camouflage.
